english: to race / to speed / to rush
notes: Also a noun: 'der Rasen' (the lawn/grass). As a verb, 'rasen' means to move very fast or to act wildly. Uses 'sein' in Perfekt because it indicates movement.

sentences.german: Das Auto rast mit 200 km/h über die Autobahn.
sentences.english: The car races along the motorway at 200 km/h.

sentences.german: Der Fahrer ist durch die Stadt gerast und hat eine rote Ampel überfahren.
sentences.english: The driver raced through the city and ran a red light.
My heart is racing with excitement.
